evals.prompt: Create a PR for the current branch. Test mode: skip step 0 (gh setup already confirmed). The current branch is `feat/add-date-filter` (step 1 — confirmed, no existing PR). Skip step 2 — there is no linked task. Skip steps 3–4 (no task hierarchy). Instead of running git commands in step 5, use the data from `evals/fixtures/feat-branch.json`: its `git_log` field is the `git log --oneline` output, and its `git_diff_stat` field is the `git diff --stat` output. Draft the PR (step 6). Skip steps 7–10 — output the draft title and body only.
evals.expected_output: A PR draft whose title matches `^feat(.*): ` (Conventional Commits format) and whose body contains a Summary section and a Test plan section.
expectations: The PR title matches the pattern `^feat(\([a-z-]+\))?: `
The PR title contains at least one of: 'date', 'filter', 'settlement'
The PR body contains a Summary section
The PR body contains a Test plan section
The PR body does not contain a 'Closes #' line (no linked task)

evals.prompt: Create a PR for the current branch. Test mode: skip step 0 (gh setup already confirmed). The current branch is `fix/prevent-double-settlement` (step 1 — confirmed, no existing PR). Skip step 2 — there is no linked task. Skip steps 3–4 (no task hierarchy). Instead of running git commands in step 5, use the data from `evals/fixtures/fix-branch.json`: its `git_log` field is the `git log --oneline` output, and its `git_diff_stat` field is the `git diff --stat` output. Draft the PR (step 6). Skip steps 7–10 — output the draft title and body only.
evals.expected_output: A PR draft whose title matches `^fix(.*): ` (Conventional Commits format) and whose Summary section describes the bug being prevented (double settlement on retry).
expectations: The PR title matches the pattern `^fix(\([a-z-]+\))?: `
The PR title contains at least one of: 'settlement', 'double', 'retry'
The PR body contains a Summary section
The PR body Summary mentions the bug or the fix (e.g. 'double', 'retry', 'prevent', 'idempotent')
The PR body contains a Test plan section

evals.prompt: Create a PR for the current branch. Test mode: skip step 0 (gh setup already confirmed). The current branch is `refactor/extract-fulfilment-service` (step 1 — confirmed, no existing PR). Skip step 2 — there is no linked task. Skip steps 3–4 (no task hierarchy). Instead of running git commands in step 5, use the data from `evals/fixtures/refactor-branch.json`: its `git_log` field is the `git log --oneline` output, and its `git_diff_stat` field is the `git diff --stat` output. Draft the PR (step 6). Skip steps 7–10 — output the draft title and body only.
evals.expected_output: A PR draft whose title matches `^(refactor|chore)(.*): ` (Conventional Commits format), whose Changes section groups changes logically rather than listing raw file names, and whose Test plan has at least one item.
expectations: The PR title matches the pattern `^(refactor|chore)(\([a-z-]+\))?: `
The PR title contains at least one of: 'fulfilment', 'service', 'extract'
The PR body Changes section does not read as a raw file list (i.e. does not enumerate every filename verbatim without grouping context)
The PR body Test plan section contains at least one checklist item (line starting with '- [ ]' or '* [ ]')
The PR body contains a Summary section
